findings.description: _hits is an unbounded defaultdict keyed by client identity with no max size, TTL, or internal scheduling — cleanup depends entirely on an external caller invoking sweep(), so memory grows linearly with unique client keys (~3 KB per full bucket) between sweeps, and sweep itself is a stop-the-world O(all buckets x bucket size) scan.

Window pruning uses list.pop(0) in a while loop on the per-request hot path — each pop is an O(n) memmove making pruning O(k*limit) per check and O(limit^2) as the configured limit grows; use collections.deque.popleft() (O(1)) or bisect_left plus a single del hits[:i].

current_usage reads via self._hits[key] on a defaultdict, so every probe of an unknown client permanently inserts an empty list — a read-only monitoring method that leaks memory on every miss until the next sweep; use self._hits.get(key, ()).

No synchronization: in a threaded gateway, concurrent check() calls can race the hits-nonempty test against pop(0) (IndexError) and sweep() can delete a bucket while check() appends to the orphaned list (silent counter reset), while the naive fix of one global lock would serialize all requests — use sharded/per-key locks.

A synchronous WARNING is logged for every rejected request, so the over-limit path — by definition the highest-volume path under abuse — amplifies into log flooding and disk I/O pressure; sample or rate-limit the log line itself.

sweep()'s any(t >= cutoff for t in hits) scans the ascending timestamp list oldest-first and walks every expired entry before short-circuiting; since the list is append-sorted, hits[-1] >= cutoff answers in O(1), reducing sweep from O(total timestamps) to O(buckets).

Client key trusts the first attacker-controlled X-Forwarded-For hop, enabling rate-limit bypass and key-cardinality memory exhaustion via spoofed headers — noted briefly here, detail deferred to critic-security.
Off-by-one: len(hits) <= self.limit admits limit+1 requests per window and yields Decision.remaining = -1 at the boundary — noted briefly, detail deferred to critic-logic.
time.time() wall clock is used for interval arithmetic, so an NTP step shifts the window (entries expiring early or never); time.monotonic() is correct for durations — deferred to critic-logic.
